meaning: I came across / I stumbled upon
communicative_purpose: To describe finding something unexpectedly while looking for or doing something else
trigger_situations: Finding interesting content, discovering things online, unexpected encounters
contexts: Social media browsing, research, random discoveries, articles
variations: je suis tombé sur, je suis tombée sur, je suis tombée sur cette info, je suis tombée sur un article
common_mistakes: Agreement with speaker gender ('tombé' masculine / 'tombée' feminine); past tense usage
collocates: cette info, cette vidéo, cette personne, cette photo
example_1: Je suis tombé sur une vidéo super drôle en scrollant Insta.
example_2: Je suis tombée sur cet article par hasard ce matin.
example_3: T'es déjà tombé sur des fake news comme ça ?
example_1_translation: I came across a super funny video while scrolling through Insta.
example_2_translation: I came across this article by chance this morning.
example_3_translation: Have you ever come across fake news like this?

communicative_purpose: To express strong approval and enthusiasm for something
trigger_situations: Recommending things, expressing amazement, praising quality
contexts: Movies, music, food, products, experiences, social media
variations: c'est de la bombe, c'est une vraie bombe, c'est bin炸弹, c'est trop bien
common_mistakes: English mixing with 'bomb'; should use more French expressions in formal contexts
collocates: cette chanson, ce restau, ce film, ce produit
example_1: Ce dernier album de jazz, c'est de la bombe !
example_2: Le concert d'hier soir, c'était de la vraie bombe.
example_3: Cette recette de sushi, c'est de la bombe atomique !
example_1_translation: This latest jazz album is the bomb!
example_2_translation: Last night's concert was a real bomb.
example_3_translation: This sushi recipe is the atomic bomb!

meaning: I'm team / I'm on the side of
communicative_purpose: To express which side or option one supports in a debate or choice
trigger_situations: Taking sides, expressing preferences, choosing between options
contexts: Fan debates, choices, opinions, sports teams, relationship drama
variations: je suis team, je suis team X, team Y, je suis team confiture
common_mistakes: English expression used in French; can be confused with actual sports teams
example_1: Entre les deux finales, je suis team chocolat à fond.
example_2: Je suis team repos ce week-end, j'ai besoin de décompresser.
example_3: Tu es teamNetflix ou team sortie ce soir ?
example_1_translation: Between the two finals, I am fully team chocolate.
example_2_translation: I'm on team rest this weekend, I need to unwind.
example_3_translation: Are you teamNetflix or team outing tonight?

communicative_purpose: To express that something is nonsense, absurd, or makes no sense
trigger_situations: Reacting to absurd claims, nonsense arguments, crazy statements
contexts: Debates, gossip, media commentary, absurd claims
variations: c'est du nawak, c'est nawak, c'est du grand nawak, c'est du n'importe quoi
collocates: total, absolute, ce mec, cette personne
example_1: Les théories conspirationnistes sur TikTok, c'est du nawak.
example_2: Ce qu'il raconte, c'est nawak complet, crois rien.
example_3: Le prix qu'ils demandent pour ça, c'est du grand nawak.
example_1_translation: Conspiracy theories on TikTok are nonsense.
example_2_translation: What he says is complete nawak, don't believe it.
example_3_translation: The price they ask for that is big nawak.

communicative_purpose: To state that one follows someone on social media
trigger_situations: Talking about social media, subscriptions, keeping up with content
contexts: Social media, influencers, subscriptions, online presence
variations: je suis follower, je follow, je suis abonné, je suis un follower
common_mistakes: English word mixing; French would say 'abonné' or 'je suis abonnée'
example_1: Je suis follower de cette chaîne YouTube depuis 2020.
example_2: Je follow son compte Insta, ses photos sont beautés.
example_3: Tu es follower de qui sur Twitter pour être si bien informé ?
example_1_translation: I have been a follower of this YouTube channel since 2020.
example_2_translation: I follow her Insta account, her photos are beautiful.
example_3_translation: Who do you follow on Twitter to be so well informed?

meaning: it's been seen before / it's nothing new
communicative_purpose: To indicate that something is not original, has been seen before
trigger_situations: Critiquing lack of originality, recognizing clichés, media criticism
contexts: Movies, TV shows, trends, music, memes, content criticism
variations: c'est du vu, c'est déjà vu, c'est du réchauffé, c'est repeating
common_mistakes: Using 'vu' alone when full expression needed; mixing with 'c'est évident'
collocates: partout, avant, toujours, dans les films
example_1: Cette histoire d'amour dans le film, c'est du vu et revu.
example_2: Les blagues du comedian, c'est déjà vu, pas drôle.
example_3: Ce concept de emission, c'est du vu dans tous les pays.
example_1_translation: This love story in the film is seen again and again.
example_2_translation: The comedian's jokes are deja vu, not funny.
example_3_translation: This concept of emissions is seen in all countries.

communicative_purpose: To express support for a particular side, team, or option
trigger_situations: Shipping debates, sports discussions, choosing sides, preferences
contexts: Fan communities, sports, TV drama debates, preference polls
variations: je suis team, je suis team X, team pour, je suis team acheteuse
common_mistakes: English expression now common in French; use 'équipe' for literal sports
collocates: pour, contre, au-dessus, dans le fond
example_1: Je suis team repos ce week-end, j'ai vraiment besoin de dormir.
example_2: Team café serré ou team café long ? Je suis team serré.
example_3: Dans le débat du final, je suis team justice sociale.
example_1_translation: I'm on team rest this weekend, I really need to sleep.
example_2_translation: Tight coffee team or long coffee team? I'm a tight team.
example_3_translation: In the final debate, I am team social justice.

communicative_purpose: To refer to media content, videos, posts, or creative material
trigger_situations: Discussing media, content creation, social media, YouTube
contexts: Content creation, media criticism, platform discussions, influencer culture
variations: c'est du contenu, du bon contenu, du mauvais contenu, du contenu de qualité
common_mistakes: Overusing 'contenu' when more specific terms exist (vidéo, article, post)
collocates: bon, mauvais, de qualité, gratuit, premium
example_1: Cette chaîne propose du bon contenu éducatif sur la programmation.
example_2: Trop de contenu sponsorisé sur Instagram, c'est usant.
example_3: Je cherche du contenu gratuit pour apprendre la photo.
example_1_translation: This channel offers good educational content on programming.
example_2_translation: Too much sponsored content on Instagram is tiring.
example_3_translation: I'm looking for free content to learn photography.

communicative_purpose: To express that one likes or has liked something on social media
trigger_situations: Social media interactions, expressing approval, showing engagement
contexts: Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube, online interactions
variations: je like, je liked, je mets un like, je likecomment
common_mistakes: English verb being used in French; French would say 'liker' or 'aimer'
collocates: ce post, cette photo, cette vidéo, ce commentaire
example_1: Je like toujours tes posts, ils sont créatifs !
example_2: Tu as liké ma story ? Je vois que t'as liké.
example_3: Si le contenu est bien, je like sans hésiter.
example_1_translation: I always like your posts, they are creative!
example_2_translation: Did you like my story? I see you liked it.
example_3_translation: If the content is good, I will like it without hesitation.

communicative_purpose: To describe content designed to attract clicks without substance
trigger_situations: Critiquing media, recognizing manipulative headlines, media literacy
contexts: Online media criticism, YouTube thumbnails, headlines, attention economics
variations: c'est du clic, c'est du clickbait, c'est pour le clic, c'est racoleur
common_mistakes: English mixing; more French expression would be 'racleur' or 'tape-à-l'œil'
example_1: Le titre de cette vidéo, c'est du clic mais le contenu est nul.
example_2: Ces articles buzzfeed, c'est du clic sans substance.
example_3: Le thumbnail racoleur, c'est fait pour le clic,点进去就是浪费.
example_1_translation: The title of this video is clicky but the content sucks.
example_2_translation: These buzzfeed articles are clicks without substance.
example_3_translation: The eye-catching thumbnail is made for clicking.

communicative_purpose: To identify as someone who streams content live on platforms
trigger_situations: Streaming culture, influencer talk, gaming, live content
contexts: Twitch, YouTube Live, gaming, influencer culture, live streaming
variations: je suis streamer, je suis streamriceuse, je stream, je fais du streaming
common_mistakes: Gender agreement; 'streamer' works but 'streamer' is increasingly used as French noun
collocates: sur Twitch, le dimanche, des jeux, en live
example_1: Je suis streamer sur Twitch, je joue aux jeux les week-ends.
example_2: Je stream mes sessions de lecture pour motiver mes followers.
example_3: Tu connais des streamers français de travel vlogging ?
example_1_translation: I'm a Twitch streamer, I play games on the weekends.
example_2_translation: I stream my reading sessions to motivate my followers.
example_3_translation: Do you know any French travel vlogging streamers?

meaning: it's recycled / it's been done before
communicative_purpose: To criticize something as unoriginal or rehashed content
trigger_situations: Critiquing remakes, sequels, rip-offs, lack of creativity
contexts: Movies, TV shows, music, formulas, media criticism
variations: c'est du réchauffé, c'est du réchauffé de, c'est réchauffé, c'est la même sauce
common_mistakes: Using 'réchauffé' alone without context; mixing with 'c'est répétitif'
collocates: de cette formule, du même film, des mêmes blagues, encore une fois
example_1: Ce nouveau film Marvel, c'est du réchauffé des anciens.
example_2: Les blagues stand-up de ce comedian, c'est du réchauffé de son dernier spectacle.
example_3: Cette tendance TikTok, c'est du réchauffé de 2019.
example_1_translation: This new Marvel film is a rehash of the old ones.
example_2_translation: This comedian's stand-up jokes are rehashes from his last show.
example_3_translation: This TikTok trend is a rehash of 2019.

communicative_purpose: To describe watching multiple episodes or content in one session
trigger_situations: TV habits, series watching, weekend plans, streaming habits
contexts: Netflix, series watching, streaming platforms, TV habits
variations: je binge watch, je binge-watch, je binge, je me fais une session série
common_mistakes: English expression now commonly used in French; French alternative 'regarder en rafale'
collocates: des séries, une saison entière, Netflix, ce week-end
example_1: J'ai binge-watché trois saisons de cette série ce week-end.
example_2: Le nouvel épisode sort ce soir, tu vas binge ?
example_3: Je me fais une session binge-watch ce soir, rien d'autre de prévu.
example_1_translation: I binge-watched three seasons of this series this weekend.
example_2_translation: The new episode comes out tonight, are you going to binge?
example_3_translation: I'm having a binge-watch session this evening, nothing else planned.

meaning: it's good stuff / this is quality
communicative_purpose: To express that something is good quality or excellent
trigger_situations: Recommending quality content, approving of things, praising
contexts: Products, content, services, music, media, recommendations
variations: c'est du bon, c'est du bon matos, c'est de la bonne, c'est du lourd
common_mistakes: Confusing 'du bon' with 'du bon temps' (good time); gender agreement issues
example_1: Ce podcast sur la science, c'est du bon, j'apprends beaucoup.
example_2: Le restau推荐 de Paul, c'était du bon, merci mec !
example_3: Le travail de cet artisan, c'est du bon matos.
example_1_translation: This science podcast is good, I'm learning a lot.
example_2_translation: Paul's restaurant was good, thanks man!
example_3_translation: The work of this craftsman is good stuff.

communicative_purpose: To describe being an online observer who watches but doesn't interact
trigger_situations: Online behavior discussions, community membership, passive consumption
contexts: Reddit, forums, social media, online communities, observation
variations: je suis lurker, je lurke, je regarde sans interagir, je suis un lurker
common_mistakes: English term used in French online communities; more French 'je suis un observateur'
collocates: sur ce sub, sur ce forum, depuis longtemps, sans compte
example_1: Je suis lurker sur Reddit depuis des années, j'ai jamais posté.
example_2: Je lurke les stories de mes ex sans interagir.
example_3: La plupart des gens sont des lurkers, ils regardent mais participent jamais.
example_1_translation: I've been a lurker on Reddit for years, never posted.
example_2_translation: I lurk my ex's stories without interacting.
example_3_translation: Most people are lurkers, they watch but never participate.
